steps: Collect the monthly download CSV for each show into one folder on your computer.
Open Claude Code and point it at that folder.
Ask it to merge the CSVs and chart downloads per episode and per show over time.
Ask it to flag which episodes outperformed their own show's median.
Ask it to state, in plain words, exactly how it's defining a "download" — and keep that definition attached to the report.
Write the result into one workbook you can point Claude Code at again next month, instead of starting over.
verify: Pick one show's total from the workbook and add up that show's downloads yourself straight from its monthly CSVs — if your total doesn't match, check whether the merge dropped a month or double-counted one.
